info.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud

location.description: Location where the resource is stored

Common.CloudError.description: Common error response for all Azure Resource Manager APIs to return error details for failed operations. (This also follows the OData error response format.).

Common.ErrorAdditionalInfo.description: The resource management error additional info.

Common.ProvisioningState.description: The current provisioning state of the resource. Indicates the status of the last operation performed on the resource.

values.name: Succeeded
values.description: The resource has been successfully provisioned and is ready for use.

values.name: Creating
values.description: The resource is being created. This is a transitional state.

values.name: Updating
values.description: The resource is being updated. This is a transitional state.

values.name: Deleting
values.description: The resource is being deleted. This is a transitional state.

values.name: Failed
values.description: The last operation on the resource failed. Check the error details for more information.

values.name: Canceled
values.description: The operation was canceled before completion.

ConnectableResource.description: Describes the allowed inbound and outbound traffic of an Azure resource

inboundConnectedResources.description: The list of Azure resources that the resource has inbound allowed connection from

outboundConnectedResources.description: The list of Azure resources that the resource has outbound allowed connection to

connectedResourceId.description: The Azure resource id of the connected resource

ConnectedWorkspace.description: Represents an OMS workspace to which the solution is connected

id.description: Azure resource ID of the connected OMS workspace

location.description: Location where the resource is stored

securityFamily.description: The security family of the discovered solution

ExternalSecuritySolution.description: Represents a security solution external to Microsoft Defender for Cloud which sends information to an OMS workspace and whose data is displayed by Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

properties.description: The resource-specific properties for this resource.

kind.description: The kind of the external solution

location.description: Location where the resource is stored

ExternalSecuritySolutionKind.description: The kind of the external solution

ExternalSecuritySolutionProperties.description: The solution properties (correspond to the solution kind)

workspace.description: Represents an OMS workspace to which the solution is connected

JitNetworkAccessPolicy.description: Concrete proxy resource types can be created by aliasing this type using a specific property type.

location.description: Location where the resource is stored

allowedSourceAddressPrefix.description: Source of the allowed traffic. If omitted, the request will be for the source IP address of the initiate request.

endTimeUtc.description: The time to close the request in UTC

virtualMachines.description: A list of virtual machines & ports to open access for

justification.description: The justification for making the initiate request

id.description: Resource ID of the virtual machine that is linked to this policy

ports.description: The ports to open for the resource with the `id`

provisioningState.description: Gets the provisioning state of the Just-in-Time policy.

id.description: Resource ID of the virtual machine that is linked to this policy

ports.description: Port configurations for the virtual machine

publicIpAddress.description: Public IP address of the Azure Firewall that is linked to this policy, if applicable

allowedSourceAddressPrefix.description: Mutually exclusive with the "allowedSourceAddressPrefixes" parameter. Should be an IP address or CIDR, for example "192.168.0.3" or "192.168.0.0/16".

maxRequestAccessDuration.description: Maximum duration requests can be made for. In ISO 8601 duration format. Minimum 5 minutes, maximum 1 day

startTimeUtc.description: The start time of the request in UTC

requestor.description: The identity of the person who made the request

justification.description: The justification for making the initiate request

allowedSourceAddressPrefix.description: Mutually exclusive with the "allowedSourceAddressPrefixes" parameter. Should be an IP address or CIDR, for example "192.168.0.3" or "192.168.0.0/16".

endTimeUtc.description: The date & time at which the request ends in UTC

statusReason.description: A description of why the `status` has its value

mappedPort.description: The port which is mapped to this port's `number` in the Azure Firewall, if applicable

id.description: Resource ID of the virtual machine that is linked to this policy

ports.description: The ports that were opened for the virtual machine

SecurityFamily.description: The security family of the discovered solution

SecuritySolution.description: Concrete proxy resource types can be created by aliasing this type using a specific property type.

location.description: Location where the resource is stored

securityFamily.description: The security family of the security solution

StatusReason.description: A description of why the `status` has its value

TopologyResource.description: Concrete proxy resource types can be created by aliasing this type using a specific property type.

location.description: Location where the resource is stored

calculatedDateTime.description: The UTC time on which the topology was calculated

topologyResources.description: Azure resources which are part of this topology resource

severity.description: The security severity of the resource

recommendationsExist.description: Indicates if the resource has security recommendations

networkZones.description: Indicates the resource connectivity level to the Internet (InternetFacing, Internal ,etc.)

topologyScore.description: Score of the resource based on its security severity

parents.description: Azure resources connected to this resource which are in higher level in the topology view

children.description: Azure resources connected to this resource which are in lower level in the topology view

resourceId.description: Azure resource id which serves as child resource in topology view
Azure resource id which serves as parent resource in topology view

id.description: Fully qualified resource ID for the resource. Ex - /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/{resourceProviderNamespace}/{resourceType}/{resourceName}

type.description: The type of the resource. E.g. "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines" or "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts"
